globalNews.title: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
globalNews.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

globalNews.title: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
globalNews.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

globalNews.title: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
globalNews.summary: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

globalNews.title: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
globalNews.summary: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

globalNews.title: Bodies of 50 infants dumped at Trinidad graveyard
globalNews.summary: Police say a preliminary investigation shows it may be a case of an "unlawful disposal of unclaimed corpses".

cryptoNews.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
cryptoNews.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

cryptoNews.title: Bitcoin price drops to $75K as new Hormuz closure puts focus on oil
cryptoNews.summary: Bitcoin foreshadows fresh market mayhem as it appears that the US-Iran war has returned, including the closure of the Strait of Hormuz oil route.

cryptoNews.title: Aluminum giant Alcoa to sell dormant smelter to Bitcoin miner NYDIG: Report
cryptoNews.summary: Alcoa is nearing a deal to sell its idle Massena East smelter to NYDIG as US industrial sites increasingly shift toward Bitcoin mining and AI data centers.

cryptoNews.title: RaveDAO denies manipulation as Binance, Bitget probe RAVE trading activity
cryptoNews.summary: RaveDAO denies involvement in RAVE token price surge and crash as Binance and Bitget launch investigations into suspected market manipulation.

cryptoNews.title: Charles Schwab, Citadel Securities weigh entering prediction markets
cryptoNews.summary: Charles Schwab and Citadel Securities executives have separately aired interest in prediction markets, but each is looking to steer clear of sports offerings.

aiNews.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
aiNews.summary: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

aiNews.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
aiNews.summary: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

aiNews.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
aiNews.summary: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

aiNews.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
aiNews.summary: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

aiNews.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
aiNews.summary: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.
